name: Split Pea Stew with Chunky Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Sweet potatoes, peeled and cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the peas in 4 cups of water for at least 4 hours.
2. In a medium saucepan, bring the soaked peas and water to a boil.
3. Add the tomatoes and simmer, covered, until the peas are very soft, about 20 to 25 minutes.
4. Puree 2 cups of the pea mixture in a blender or food processor until smooth, adding a little extra water if necessary.
5. Return the pureed peas to the saucepan.
6. Cover and keep the stew warm.
7. In a large, deep pot, combine onion (if using), garlic, parsley, dill, sweet potatoes, and 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 6 to 7 minutes.
8. Add the broccoli and simmer another 6 to 8 minutes, or until the broccoli is tender but still crunchy.
9. Add the pureed pea mixture to the pot, reducing heat to low.
10. Cover and cook gently until just heated through.
11. Serve garnished with parsley. Serves 4.
Per serving: 271 calories; 16 grams protein; 1 gram fat; 54 grams carbohydrates; 0 cholesterol; 38 milligrams sodium; vegan
Source: Vegetarian Times, January 1992/January 2022 by Deanne
